steps.title: Identify the critical timeline detail
steps.content: Text 2 reveals that Deccan gases affected climate, but “the climate returned to normal well before the extinctions began.” This timeline directly contradicts the underlined argument's claim that volcanic activity caused the extinction.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Since volcanic climate effects ended before extinctions occurred, they couldn't have caused the extinction.

Hull's team responds by declaring that the Deccan climate changes weren't the main cause of K-Pg extinction—which is exactly what Choice B states.

The other options either agree with the dissenters (A), misconstrue the scientific disagreement (C), or misrepresent Hull's findings (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
